content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory architectures can be retrofitted to tag and encode failed exploration trajectories as self-supervised 'contrastive memory anchors', where negative results from uninformative actions are stored as distinct episodic memory clusters that the agent explicitly queries to avoid redundant active learning queries. This turns unused failed trajectory data into a free source of self-supervised signal that lowers redundant sampling and boosts sample efficiency for curiosity-driven active agents.
source_topics: Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
